title: Yoga and Cognition: A Meta-Analysis of Chronic and Acute Effects
headline: Yoga and Cognition: A Meta-Analysis of Chronic and Acute Effects
summary: A meta-analysis / systematic review of yoga examined focus & attention. Yoga practice seems to be associated with moderate improvements in cognitive function.
keyFindings: Yoga practice seems to be associated with moderate improvements in cognitive function.
limitations: The studies varied widely in method and population, so the combined estimate blurs real differences between them.
The evidence base is small, so the finding is preliminary.
Pooled estimates combine heterogeneous studies; effect sizes vary by practice, population, and control condition.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Yoga and Cognition: A Meta-Analysis of Chronic and Acute Effects
aiSnippet: Meta-analysis / Systematic review, Psychosomatic medicine (2015). Yoga practice seems to be associated with moderate improvements in cognitive function.
mechanismGap: The practice in this study does not share a specific breath-regulation or directed-attention mechanism with the Miracle of Mind practice, so no practice bridge is drawn here.
